compiled_state.title: Add sub-agent guard to skills that require sub-agents or user interaction
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context
When orchestrator skills like /dso:sprint or /dso:debug-everything dispatch sub-agents that invoke other skills via the Skill tool, those nested skills silently degrade — they can't dispatch their own sub-agents (hitting nesting failures) or conduct user interaction (no user is present). Today, only /dso:fix-bug detects this and adapts. The remaining 18 skills that depend on sub-agents or user interaction proceed blindly, producing low-quality results or outright failures that are difficult to diagnose.

## Success Criteria
- Each of the 18 target skills exits immediately with a clear error message when it detects it is running as a sub-agent
- Skills that require sub-agents use Agent tool availability as the detection method
- Skills that require user interaction use the orchestrator signal phrase ("You are running as a sub-agent") as the detection method
- The guard block follows a consistent pattern across all 18 skills (same structure, same error message format)
- No existing orchestrator workflow breaks — no current workflow legitimately invokes these skills from sub-agent context
- The guard appears in the skill preamble (before any other logic) so no work is wasted before exiting

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Add a ~10-line guard block to the preamble of each target skill. Two variants: one for skills needing sub-agents (checks Agent tool availability), one for skills needing user interaction (checks for orchestrator signal phrase). Plain error message on detection — no structured report format.

## Target Skills
Sub-agent dependent (15): sprint, debug-everything, brainstorm, preplanning, implementation-plan, design-wireframe, design-review, roadmap, plan-review, review-protocol, resolve-conflicts, dev-onboarding, validate-work, retro, ui-discover
User-interaction dependent (3): end-session, project-setup, design-onboarding
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context (revised — supersedes original)

When orchestrator skills dispatch sub-agents that invoke other skills via the Skill tool, those nested skills silently degrade — they can't dispatch their own sub-agents or conduct user interaction. Additionally, sprint's invocation of implementation-plan as a Task tool sub-agent prevents implementation-plan from executing review-protocol, resulting in unreviewed implementation plans. A Claude Code limitation caps nested skill invocation at 2 levels, so the sprint/implementation-plan/review-protocol chain must be flattened.

## Success Criteria (revised — supersedes original)

- Each of the 18 target skills exits immediately with a clear error message when it detects it is running as a sub-agent
- Skills that require sub-agents use Agent tool availability as the detection method
- Skills that require user interaction use the orchestrator signal phrase as the detection method
- The guard block follows a consistent pattern across all 18 skills
- The guard appears in the skill preamble (before any other logic)
- Review-protocol logic is extracted into REVIEW-PROTOCOL-WORKFLOW.md and the review-protocol skill becomes a thin wrapper that reads and executes it
- Implementation-plan reads and executes REVIEW-PROTOCOL-WORKFLOW.md inline instead of invoking the review-protocol skill
- Sprint invokes implementation-plan via Skill tool (not Task tool), eliminating the nesting that prevented plan reviews
- Implementation plans produced by sprint are reviewed via the review protocol workflow

## Approach (revised — supersedes original)

Two parallel tracks: (1) Add ~10-line guard blocks to 18 skill preambles with two variants (Agent tool check, orchestrator signal check). (2) Extract review-protocol into a workflow document, make the skill a thin wrapper, update implementation-plan to execute it inline, and update sprint to invoke implementation-plan via Skill tool instead of Task tool.

## Target Skills (unchanged)
Sub-agent dependent (15): sprint, debug-everything, brainstorm, preplanning, implementation-plan, design-wireframe, design-review, roadmap, plan-review, review-protocol, resolve-conflicts, dev-onboarding, validate-work, retro, ui-discover
User-interaction dependent (3): end-session, project-setup, design-onboarding
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Epic complete: all 6 children closed. All 18 skills now have SUB-AGENT-GUARD blocks. Implementation-plan migrated to inline review-protocol-workflow. Sprint migrated to Skill tool invocation for implementation-plan.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
